channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tren de Aragua gang members arrested in police raid at Texas apartment complex
item.description: San Antonio Police Chief William McManus confirmed four Tren de Aragua members were arrested during a raid on a vacant apartment complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Capitol Police officer recounts Jan. 6: 'It's hard to relive'
item.description: Former Capitol Hill Police Officer Winston Pingeon recalled the events of Jan. 6, 2021, when rioters breached the Capitol in an apparent attempt to stop Congress’s certification of the Electoral College results, in an interview with ABC News. "It's hard to relive and talk about moments like this.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A week after Helene hit, thousands still without water struggle to find enough
item.description: Nearly a week after Hurricane Helene brought devastation to western North Carolina, a shiny stainless steel tanker truck in downtown Asheville attracted residents carrying 5-gallon containers, milk jugs and buckets to fill with what has become a desperately scare resource — drinking water.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, JEFF AMY and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Jersey sisters battle stage 4 colon cancer together
item.description: They share a lot more than their sister bond. They are both in their late 30s and currently battling stage four colon cancer.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 Florida cities home to the worst drivers in the U.S., study says
item.description: A Tennessee city has once again been given the dubious title of home to the worst drivers in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: False and misleading claims about Hurricane Helene are spreading about the storm and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Supreme Court opens its new term with election disputes in the air but not yet on the docket
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Transgender rights, the regulation of “ghost guns” and the death penalty highlight the Supreme Court's election-season term that begins Monday, with the prospect of the court's intervention in voting disputes lurking in the background.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minnesota boy arrested after allegedly driving stolen car near playground where children were playing
item.description: The 10-year-old boy, who reportedly has a history of auto theft and threats of violence, was arrested in Minneapolis.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawyer for Diddy child accuser vows to out famous accomplices 'we all know'
item.description: The Texas lawyer who says he's representing more than dozens of potential victims of alleged sex trafficking music mogul Sean "Diddy" Combs vowed to name names in an ominous warning this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Well-known Asheville music tradition returns in a sign of hopefulness after Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — A well-known Asheville musical tradition returned Friday night, in a sign of hopefulness a week after Helene battered the mountain city. The Asheville Drum Circle had its first regular Friday night session since the powerful storm blew in.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, KRON4

item.title: North Carolina is distributing Benadryl and EpiPens as yellow jackets swarm from Helene flooding
item.description: Deadly flooding from Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina has also disrupted the underground nests of yellow jackets, bees and other insects, causing them to swarm and sting people struggling to recover from the storm.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYC Deputy Mayor Sheena Wright expected to resign: Sources
item.description: Wright joins a growing list of members of Mayor Eric Adams’ inner circle who have resigned. Banks will step down from his position on Oct. 16.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump returns to site of attempted assassination, honors shooting victims
item.description: Former President Donald Trump is returning Saturday to the site of his attempted assassination with a rally in Butler County.
item.dc:creator: Patty Coller, Fox 5

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden: 'I don't know' if Netanyahu trying to influence election
item.description: President Biden on Friday said he wasn't aware of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu was trying to influence the U.S. election, while noting no administration has been a stronger ally to Israel than his own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Giant bear claws out from under Southern California home
item.description: Bear break-ins in the San Gabriel foothill communities are common and most homeowners have learned to cope with the big, furry nuisances.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court declines to block Biden limits on methane, toxic pollution
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday declined to block Biden administration limits on planet-warming methane from oil and gas production and toxic pollution from coal plants.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: What is Marburg virus? Outbreak of Ebola-like disease detected in yet another country
item.description: Health officials in Rwanda are dealing with the country’s first outbreak of the Marburg virus, an Ebola-like disease which, if left untreated, has a fatality rate of up to 88%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Some Republicans embrace ‘pro-choice’ label, infuriating Democrats
item.description: A few Republicans running for competitive or Democratic-leaning congressional seats are adopting and reviving a label that was nearly extinct in their party: pro-choice.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates urge Ohio to restore voter registrations removed in apparent violation of federal law
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Voter advocacy organizations have put Ohio's Republican elections chief on notice that voters are being systematically removed from the rolls in several counties in apparent violation of federal law.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to 'reevaluate' Menendez brothers' convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón addressed the public Thursday amid calls for his office to reexamine the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were found guilty of killing their parents in August 1989. Gascón said his office plans to do just that.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, Fox 5

item.title: Former Colorado county clerk Tina Peters sentenced to 9 years for voting data scheme
item.description: A judge ripped into a Colorado county clerk for her crimes and lies before sentencing her Thursday to nine years behind bars for a data-breach scheme spawned from the rampant false claims about voting machine fraud in the 2020 presidential race.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother in Georgia are youngest-known Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Month-old twin boys are the youngest known victims of Hurricane Helene. The twins died alongside their mother when a large tree fell through the roof of their home in Thomson, Georgia, last week.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Plastics factory employee recounts narrow escape from Hurricane Helene's flood
item.description: Impact Plastics employee Zinna Adkins stepped outside her workplace at 10:54 a.m. on Friday and captured water rising in the parking lot on her cellphone. But at that time, she said no supervisor informed employees they were free to leave.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, WFLA, NBC
